summary: Controller spec review allows APP-236 at the reviewed head: the minimal suite binds the explicit user-confirmed Harbor session intent to a narrow App process boundary, preserves Harbor as identity/session truth owner, and excludes automatic login, sensitive material, task execution, and all external writes. The minimal-path rationale correctly excludes only full-suite planning artifacts; packaged App E2E remains mandatory before user-story closeout.
reviewed_validation_summary: 2026-07-10 UTC, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run smoke`, and `git diff --check` passed. Smoke covers bodyless confirmation, invalid/missing authorization, generic owner API denial, public response redaction, token rotation and chunk-safe runtime output redaction. Deterministic review-readiness commands were executed against the installed Loom package because this App worktree has no `tools/` directory: `tools/skills_surface.py check` failed with `skills_docs_reference_sync_drift`; `tools/loom_check.py --profile source --source-surface contract-only` failed with source-surface mismatch because the installed package lacks the required source/distribution markers. Both are external Loom tool-surface defects outside APP-236 ownership and do not alter the App runtime boundary.
follow_ups: After Harbor #241 and App #236 merge, verify the returned public identity state in packaged App E2E before closing the user story.
